# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create AWS CloudFormation template for SQS queue and SNS topic setup. Define queue with message retention and visibility timeout. Set up SNS topic for pub/sub messaging. Configure queue policy to allow message operations. This is for our serverless event processing architecture.
# Category: sqs_policy_too_permissive

Resources: 
    MyQueue:
        Type: AWS::SQS::Queue   # Define a SQS Queue resource type (for your case, 'MyQueue')         
      
    NotifyTopic:                  # Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service topic to publish messages. This is defined as another CloudFormation Resource – MySNSTopic     Type - AWS::SNS::Topic   Name : !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-My-topic"      Provider: AWSSDKv2
                                     # Amazon SQS queues only accept messages from one producer. In order to allow multiple producers, we need a second Queue resource type and an IAM Role that allows it – Allow Accesses for 'AmazonSimpleNotificationServiceRole'.       Type : AWS::IAM::Role   Name: !Sub "${AWS::StackName}-My-role"    AssumeRolePolicyDocument:[ {sts_assume_role_policy.json} ]
                                     # This role allows our SQS queue to publish messages and allow message operations on the topic – AmazonSimpleNotificationServicePermissions   Type - AWS::IAM::Policy Document: |                                                                                             {"Statement": [{"Action":"sqs:Publish","Resource":{ "Ref" : MyQueue },
                                     # 'AWSLambdaBasicExecutionRole' for our SQS queue operations. It grants the necessary permissions – Execution role used by AWS Lambda (default). The JSON definition of this executionrole is provided below, as part a more complex policy-aside example: {"Version":"2012-10-17","Statement":[{"Action":"sts:AssumeRole", "Principal" : { “Service”:"lambda.amazonaws.com"}, "Effect":"Allow","Sid":"VisualEditor0"]} ]
     SQSPublishExecutionRoleArn!Sub "[![Base64:AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAAAgd+...(cut)...]" # Encode base 64 of the above role definition JSON string. It's also possible to set up this policy in a separate file
     ARN!Sub "[![Base64:AAAAB3NzaC1yc2EAAAADAQABAAAAgd+...(cut)...]" # Encode base 64 of the above role definition JSON string. It's also possible to set up this policy in a separate file
